issues.title: Incorrect table and column names
issues.line: select ***gh from my_table m leeeft join my_table2 t where m.id = t.id and m.value > 100;
issues.problem: The query uses incorrect table and column names that do not match the provided schema.
issues.fix: select t1.transaction_id, t2.merchant_name from sigma_analytics.fact_transactions t1 left join sigma_analytics.dim_merchant t2 on t1.merchant_id = t2.merchant_id where t1.amount > 100;

issues.title: Poor readability due to incorrect table and column names
issues.line: select ***gh from my_table m leeeft join my_table2 t where m.id = t.id and m.value > 100;
issues.problem: The query uses non-descriptive aliases and incorrect table and column names, making it hard to read and understand.
issues.fix: select t1.transaction_id, t2.merchant_name from sigma_analytics.fact_transactions t1 left join sigma_analytics.dim_merchant t2 on t1.merchant_id = t2.merchant_id where t1.amount > 100;

issues.title: Inconsistent spacing and typos
issues.line: select ***gh from my_table m leeeft join my_table2 t where m.id = t.id and m.value > 100;
issues.problem: The query contains inconsistent spacing and typos, such as 'leeeft' instead of 'left'.
issues.fix: select t1.transaction_id, t2.merchant_name from sigma_analytics.fact_transactions t1 left join sigma_analytics.dim_merchant t2 on t1.merchant_id = t2.merchant_id where t1.amount > 100;

review.corrected_sql: select t1.transaction_id, t2.merchant_name from sigma_analytics.fact_transactions t1 left join sigma_analytics.dim_merchant t2 on t1.merchant_id = t2.merchant_id where t1.amount > 100;
review.summary: The query had critical correctness issues due to incorrect table and column names, and readability issues due to inconsistent spacing and typos.

issues.title: Incorrect table references
issues.problem: The query references tables 'users' and 'orders' which do not exist in the provided schema. The correct tables are 'sigma_analytics.dim_customer' and 'sigma_analytics.fact_transactions'.

issues.title: Missing join condition
issues.problem: The query is using a comma-separated list of tables without an explicit JOIN clause, which can lead to a Cartesian product.
issues.fix: Use an explicit JOIN clause to join 'sigma_analytics.dim_customer' and 'sigma_analytics.fact_transactions'.

issues.title: Incorrect column references
issues.problem: The query references columns 'name', 'email', 'order_id', and 'amount' which do not exist in the provided schema. The correct columns are 'customer_name', 'email', 'transaction_id', and 'amount'.
issues.fix: Replace 'name' with 'customer_name', 'order_id' with 'transaction_id', and ensure 'amount' is correctly referenced from 'sigma_analytics.fact_transactions'.

issues.title: Correlated subquery
issues.problem: The subquery is correlated to the outer query, which can be inefficient.
issues.fix: Use a JOIN with a subquery or a window function to calculate the average amount per user.

issues.title: Lack of comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the logic, making it harder to understand.
issues.fix: Add comments to explain the purpose of the query and the logic behind the conditions.

review.corrected_sql:
SELECT dc.customer_name, dc.email, ft.transaction_id
FROM sigma_analytics.dim_customer dc
JOIN sigma_analytics.fact_transactions ft ON dc.customer_id = ft.customer_id
WHERE ft.amount > (
    SELECT AVG(ft2.amount)
    FROM sigma_analytics.fact_transactions ft2
    WHERE ft2.customer_id = dc.customer_id
);
review.summary: The query had several critical correctness issues due to incorrect table and column references. It also had a performance issue with a correlated subquery and lacked readability due to missing comments.

issues.title: Invalid table reference
issues.problem: The query references a table 'users' which does not exist in the provided schema.
issues.fix: Replace 'users' with the correct table from the schema, e.g., 'sigma_analytics.fact_transactions'.

issues.title: Incorrect WHERE clause usage
issues.problem: The 'WHERE' clause is used incorrectly without specifying a table or condition related to the schema.
issues.fix: Remove the 'WHERE age > 21' clause and replace it with a valid condition from the schema.

issues.title: Lack of comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the logic or purpose.
issues.fix: Add comments to explain the purpose and logic of the query.

review.corrected_sql: SELECT ft.transaction_id, ft.amount, ft.status, ft.merchant_id, ft.customer_id, ft.transaction_date, ft.payment_method, dm.merchant_name, dc.customer_name, dc.email, dc.tier, dc.signup_date, dc.city FROM sigma_analytics.fact_transactions ft JOIN sigma_analytics.dim_merchant dm ON ft.merchant_id = dm.merchant_id JOIN sigma_analytics.dim_customer dc ON ft.customer_id = dc.customer_id WHERE ft.status = 'COMPLETED';
review.summary: The query had critical correctness issues due to invalid table references and an incorrect WHERE clause. Readability was also lacking due to the absence of comments.

issues.title: Cross Join without Join Condition
issues.problem: The query performs a cross join between customers and orders without a proper join condition, resulting in a Cartesian product.
issues.fix: SELECT c.customer_name, t.transaction_date FROM sigma_analytics.dim_customer c JOIN sigma_analytics.fact_transactions t ON c.customer_id = t.customer_id;

issues.title: Unclear Alias Usage
issues.problem: The aliases 'customers' and 'orders' are not clearly defined in the query, making it hard to understand the source tables.
issues.fix: SELECT c.customer_name, t.transaction_date FROM sigma_analytics.dim_customer c JOIN sigma_analytics.fact_transactions t ON c.customer_id = t.customer_id;

issues.title: Implicit Join
issues.problem: Using a comma-separated list in the FROM clause is an implicit join, which can lead to performance issues and is less readable.
issues.fix: SELECT c.customer_name, t.transaction_date FROM sigma_analytics.dim_customer c JOIN sigma_analytics.fact_transactions t ON c.customer_id = t.customer_id;

review.corrected_sql: SELECT c.customer_name, t.transaction_date FROM sigma_analytics.dim_customer c JOIN sigma_analytics.fact_transactions t ON c.customer_id = t.customer_id;
review.summary: The query had a critical correctness issue due to a cross join without a join condition, and also had readability and performance issues due to implicit join syntax.

issues.title: Incorrect table reference
issues.problem: The query references a 'students' table which does not exist in the provided schema.

issues.title: Lack of comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining its purpose or logic.
issues.fix:
-- Select top 5 merchant names
SELECT merchant_name FROM sigma_analytics.dim_merchant LIMIT 5;

review.corrected_sql:
-- Select top 5 merchant names
SELECT merchant_name FROM sigma_analytics.dim_merchant ORDER BY merchant_name LIMIT 5;
review.summary: The query had a critical correctness issue by referencing a non-existent table and lacked readability due to missing comments.
